query: I've been doing 3 sets of 10 squats at 135 lbs for 3 weeks and it feels easy now. How should I progress?

response.reply: When a weight feels easy for 3 weeks at your target rep range, it's a clear signal to progress. The most reliable method is linear progression: add 5–10 lbs to the bar each session (or each week for intermediate lifters). For squats at 135 lbs doing 3×10, you could move to 145 lbs next session and keep the reps the same. Alternatively, you can progress via double progression — first increase reps to 3×12, then bump the weight back down to your new working weight. Track your sessions so you can see the trend over time.
